heading: Recognition of the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority
label: 3052 Recognition of the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (f)(5), was in the original “this Act” and was translated as reading “this title”, meaning title XII of div. FF of , to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 116–260

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The private, independent, self-regulatory, nonprofit corporation, to be known as the “Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority”, is recognized for purposes of developing and implementing a horseracing anti-doping and medication control program and a racetrack safety program for covered horses, covered persons, and covered horseraces.

children.heading: Independent members
children.label: A Independent members
children.text: Five members of the Board shall be independent members selected from outside the equine industry.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Four members of the Board shall be industry members selected from among the various equine constituencies.

children.heading: Representation of equine constituencies
children.label: ii Representation of equine constituencies
children.text: The industry members shall be representative of the various equine constituencies, and shall include not more than one industry member from any one equine constituency.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: 2 Chair
children.text: The chair of the Board shall be an independent member described in paragraph (1)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: the administrative structure and employees of the Authority;

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of standing committees;

children.label: C
children.text: the procedures for filling vacancies on the Board and the standing committees;

children.label: D
children.text: term limits for members and termination of membership; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other matter the Board considers necessary.

children.heading: Anti-doping and medication control standing committee
children.label: 1 Anti-doping and medication control standing committee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Authority shall establish an anti-doping and medication control standing committee, which shall provide advice and guidance to the Board on the development and maintenance of the horseracing anti-doping and medication control program.

children.heading: Independent members
children.label: i Independent members
children.text: A majority of the members shall be independent members selected from outside the equine industry.

children.heading: Industry members
children.label: ii Industry members
children.text: A minority of the members shall be industry members selected to represent the various equine constituencies, and shall include not more than one industry member from any one equine constituency.

children.heading: Qualification
children.label: iii Qualification
children.text: A majority of individuals selected to serve on the anti-doping and medication control standing committee shall have significant, recent experience in anti-doping and medication control rules.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: C Chair
children.text: The chair of the anti-doping and medication control standing committee shall be an independent member of the Board described in subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Authority shall establish a racetrack safety standing committee, which shall provide advice and guidance to the Board on the development and maintenance of the racetrack safety program.

children.heading: Independent members
children.label: i Independent members
children.text: A majority of the members shall be independent members selected from outside the equine industry.

children.heading: Industry members
children.label: ii Industry members
children.text: A minority of the members shall be industry members selected to represent the various equine constituencies.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: C Chair
children.text: The chair of the racetrack safety standing committee shall be an industry member of the Board described in subsection (b)(1)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The nominating committee of the Authority shall be comprised of seven independent members selected from business, sports, and academia.

children.heading: Initial membership
children.label: B Initial membership
children.text: The initial nominating committee members shall be set forth in the governing corporate documents of the Authority.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: C Vacancies
children.text: After the initial committee members are appointed in accordance with subparagraph (B), vacancies shall be filled by the Board pursuant to rules established by the Authority.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: 2 Chair
children.text: The chair of the nominating committee shall be selected by the nominating committee from among the members of the nominating committee.

children.heading: Selection of members of the Board and standing committees
children.label: 3 Selection of members of the Board and standing committees

children.heading: Initial members
children.label: A Initial members
children.text: The nominating committee shall select the initial members of the Board and the standing committees described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Subsequent members
children.label: B Subsequent members
children.text: The nominating committee shall recommend individuals to fill any vacancy on the Board or on such standing committees.
An individual who has a financial interest in, or provides goods or services to, covered horses.

children.label: A
children.text: of an equine industry representative; or

children.label: B
children.text: who serves in a governance or policymaking capacity for an equine industry representative.
An employee of, or an individual who has a business or commercial relationship with, an individual described in paragraph (1) or (2).
An immediate family member of an individual described in paragraph (1) or (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Initial funding to establish the Authority and underwrite its operations before the program effective date shall be provided by loans obtained by the Authority.

children.heading: Borrowing
children.label: B Borrowing
children.text: The Authority may borrow funds toward the funding of its operations.

children.heading: Annual calculation of amounts required
children.label: C Annual calculation of amounts required
I
children.text: to fund the State’s proportionate share of the horseracing anti-doping and medication control program and the racetrack safety program for the next calendar year; and

children.label: II
children.text: to liquidate the State’s proportionate share of any loan or funding shortfall in the current calendar year and any previous calendar year.

children.label: aa
children.text: the annual budget of the Authority for the following calendar year, as approved by the Board; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the projected amount of covered racing starts for the year in each State; and

children.label: II
children.text: take into account other sources of Authority revenue.

children.heading: Requirements regarding budgets of Authority
children.label: iii Requirements regarding budgets of Authority

children.heading: Initial budget
children.label: I Initial budget
children.text: The initial budget of the Authority shall require the approval of ⅔ of the Board.

children.heading: Subsequent budgets
children.label: II Subsequent budgets
children.text: Any subsequent budget that exceeds the budget of the preceding calendar year by more than 5 percent shall require the approval of ⅔ of the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A proposed increase in the amount required under this subparagraph shall be reported to the Commission.

children.heading: Notice and comment
children.label: II Notice and comment
children.text: The Commission shall publish in the Federal Register such a proposed increase and provide an opportunity for public comment.

children.heading: Assessment and collection of fees by States
children.label: 2 Assessment and collection of fees by States

children.heading: Notice of election
children.label: A Notice of election
children.text: Any State racing commission that elects to remit fees pursuant to this subsection shall notify the Authority of such election not later than 60 days before the program effective date.

children.heading: Requirement to remit fees
children.label: B Requirement to remit fees
children.text: After a State racing commission makes a notification under subparagraph (A), the election shall remain in effect and the State racing commission shall be required to remit fees pursuant to this subsection according to a schedule established in rule developed by the Authority and approved by the Commission.

children.heading: Withdrawal of election
children.label: C Withdrawal of election
children.text: A State racing commission may cease remitting fees under this subsection not earlier than one year after notifying the Authority of the intent of the State racing commission to do so.

children.heading: Determination of methods
children.label: D Determination of methods
children.text: Each State racing commission shall determine, subject to the applicable laws, regulations, and contracts of the State, the method by which the requisite amount of fees, such as foal registration fees, sales contributions, starter fees, and track fees, and other fees on covered persons, shall be allocated, assessed, and collected.

children.heading: Assessment and collection of fees by the Authority
children.label: 3 Assessment and collection of fees by the Authority

children.heading: Calculation
children.label: A Calculation
children.text: If a State racing commission does not elect to remit fees pursuant to paragraph (2) or withdraws its election under such paragraph, the Authority shall, not less frequently than monthly, calculate the applicable fee per racing start multiplied by the number of racing starts in the State during the preceding month.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: B Allocation
children.text: The Authority shall allocate equitably the amount calculated under subparagraph (A) collected among covered persons involved with covered horseraces pursuant to such rules as the Authority may promulgate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Authority shall assess a fee equal to the allocation made under subparagraph (B) and shall collect such fee according to such rules as the Authority may promulgate.

children.heading: Remittance of fees
children.label: ii Remittance of fees
children.text: Covered persons described in subparagraph (B) shall be required to remit such fees to the Authority.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: D Limitation
children.text: A State racing commission that does not elect to remit fees pursuant to paragraph (2) or that withdraws its election under such paragraph shall not impose or collect from any person a fee or tax relating to anti-doping and medication control or racetrack safety matters for covered horseraces.

children.heading: Fees and fines
children.label: 4 Fees and fines
children.text: Fees and fines imposed by the Authority shall be allocated toward funding of the Authority and its activities.

children.label: A
children.text: the appropriation of any amount to the Authority; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Federal Government to guarantee the debts of the Authority.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: g Quorum
children.text: For all items where Board approval is required, the Authority shall have present a majority of independent members.
